name: Amish-Style Chicken and Corn Soup
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine the stewing hen with chicken stock, chopped onions, carrots, celery, and saffron threads in a large pot.
2. Bring the mixture to a simmer over medium heat.
3. Simmer for about 1 hour, skimming the surface as necessary to remove any impurities.
4. Remove the stewing hen from the pot when it is cool enough to handle. Discard the bones and pick the meat off, cutting it into neat pieces.
5. Strain the broth through a fine sieve, discarding the solids.
6. (Optional) The soup can be made up to this point in advance. Refrigerate or freeze the strained broth and diced chicken meat for 2 to 3 days, or longer if desired. When ready to use, defrost the ingredients and discard any congealed fat. Return the broth to a boil and add the diced meat. Continue with the recipe.
7. Add the corn, celery, parsley, and cooked noodles to the pot.
8. Return the soup to a simmer and serve immediately.
